title: 2. Aging and Cosmetics in TCM
text:
Perspective about aging in TCM is very interesting. TCM does not focus on the diseases
themselves. There is only the conceptual "spectrum from perfectly healthy ideal state to
death." That became a strong point of TCM with regard to preventive medicine and the
effects of aging, which had not been the target of focus in conventional medicine in old days.
Aging is not a disease, but nor it is an ideal state, so in TCM, there is a constant effort to
delay aging for longevity, but not for completely conquering of death. Therefore, restoring
your youth for cosmetics and health purposes has always been at the center of TCM.
In TCM, the decrease of the kidney function (not the exact anatomical organ of the
‘kidney’) is the cause of aging, so restoring the kidney function is the key to longevity and
cosmetic medicine. That is called Seonchun-qi, which means the qi we received from heaven
or our parents. So, the decrease of Seonchun-qi is avoidable, as it is the ﬂow of nature, but we
can help to slow down its speed by adding more Seonchun-qi. Seonchun-qi stored in the
kidney is similar to concept of hormone. TCM doctors try to add Seonchun-qi, and medical
doctors try to boost hormones the old. That is the concept of the kidney and Seonchun-qi.
~ !  ~
11
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
The second important factor is the spleen (again, not the exact anatomical organ) which
makes Hoochun-qi. Hoocun-qi is the energy we create through food and air. So it is a form of
energy that can be changed easily unlike Seonchun-qi, which is set from the start. Therefore,
treating and enhancing the spleen energy are important.
~ !  ~
12
"When a female reaches the age of 7, she has enough Kidney-qi for
the growth of permanent teeth as well as body hair. At age 14,
Chun-gye (which is similar to the concept of hormone change at
puberty)  arrives  and  she  starts  to  menstruate  and  is  able  to
conceive. At 21, the level of Kidney-qi reaches its pinnacle and her
body's development stops and her wisdom teeth grow. At 28, her
body is at its peak and she has strong muscles and bones, and thick,
rich hair. At 35, the female starts to age with a sallow face, wrinkles
or hair loss because of the Yang Ming Meridian. At 42, the three
Yang Meridians in the upper body begin to decline, which leads to
facial sagging, and hair  turning white. At 49, Chun-gye starts to
decline  and  her  body  will  then  deteriorate  overall,  and  stop
menstruation and become infertile.”
[ 黃帝內經, 素問篇, 上古天眞論篇 ]
• 女子七歲, 腎氣盛, 齒更髮長:
• 二七而天癸至, 任脉通, 太衝脉盛, 月事以時下, 故有子:
• 三七, 腎氣平均, 故眞牙生而長極:
• 四七, 筋骨堅, 髮長極, 身體盛壯:
• 五七, 陽明脉衰, 面始焦, 髮始墮:
• 六七, 三陽脉衰於上, 面皆焦, 髮始白:
• 七七, 任脉虛, 太衝脉衰少, 天癸竭, 地道不通, 故形壞而無子也.
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
The third factor is blood deﬁciency. It is not the exact blood of modern medicine. It is the
substance made by our Yin energy. Loosing hydration, saggy skin, dry skin, constipation,
hair loss, and so on are believed to happen due to blood deﬁciency. That’s why we use
various herbs and acupoints to increase blood, or Yin energy.
~ !  ~
13
Major diagnosis patterns for cosmetics
Kidney-Jing Deﬁciency
• Face and Skin:
Dark complexion, premature graying, hair loss
• Other symptoms:
Congenital weakness, weakness in the lumbar area and knees, teeth problems,
poor memory, low sex drive, infertility, tinnitus
Spleen-qi Deﬁciency
• Face and Skin:
Sallow / yellow complexion, Wrinkle formation, fat loss of the face
• Other symptoms:
Appetite loss, bloating, poor digestion, fatigue, lassitude
Blood deﬁciency
• Face and Skin:
Dry skin, hair loss, pale complexion
• Other symptoms:
Dizziness, palpitations, insomnia, poor memory, fatigue, scanty periods
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
